title: FineTuneForge — Test Report
description: Title slide introducing the end-to-end test report for FineTuneForge.
speakerNotes: Welcome. This deck walks through an end-to-end functional test of FineTuneForge, captured on May 3, 2026.

title: What We Tested
description: Overview of the modules exercised during the test pass.
speakerNotes: We exercised every primary module: landing, wizard, integrations, training history, model registry, and the in-app docs.

title: Landing & Hero
description: Screenshot of the FineTuneForge landing page with hero and wizard preview.
speakerNotes: The landing page renders cleanly with the hero, value prop, and a preview of the wizard above the fold.

title: Training Wizard
description: Task selection step showing all six task types, including LM Studio support.
speakerNotes: The wizard exposes six task types. Instruction Tuning is now flagged LM Studio Ready, matching the recent integration work.

title: Modal Integration
description: New Integrations page showing a verified Modal connection used to enable GPU training.
speakerNotes: Modal credentials are stored in-memory only, masked in the UI, and validated on save. The page shows a Connected state with verification timestamp.

title: In-App Documentation
description: Get Started page including the new Enabling GPU Training with Modal section.
speakerNotes: We added a dedicated section explaining how to enable GPU training through Modal, including where to paste the token and what the wizard expects.

title: Training Results
description: Quantitative results from the My Models registry and Job History.
speakerNotes: Top-trained models on DistilBERT reached 99.1 percent and 99.0 percent accuracy. Across 13 jobs, 4 completed successfully and 1 was actively running at capture.

title: Verdict
description: Final verdict and summary of the test pass.
speakerNotes: All primary user flows passed. Modal-backed GPU training is wired end to end and ready for production use.
